summary: Deliver the coherent M2 C2 3D-model-viewer slice on verified C9 parent 19899e6: controlled per-document camera and last-good presentation, orbit/pan/zoom and axis/fit/projection controls, themed and degradable scene furniture, off-thread cancellable STL decoding with atomic replacement and accessible fallbacks, point measurements and exact bounds, bounded project/file annotation persistence, render progress/cancel/error presentation, and scene PNG capture through the C6 artifact destination. Q-0025, Q-0026, and Q-0028 remain scoped; FR-2.5 device-class performance and visible annotation-persistence failure remain evidence/product debt. Missing original historical RED transcripts remain disclosed separately from retained reconstruction RED/GREEN evidence.
specSections: Section 2.7 questions, discretion, capability-sized unit of work, test-first evidence, and green CI
Section 4.9 C9 viewer settings consumed as the persistence authority
Section 7 N-3 keyboard accessibility and N-7 honesty
Section 8 M2 C2 viewer-complete milestone scope
Appendix D viewer camera and screenshot keybindings

decisions.topic: Per-document viewer state and fixed-size geometry identity
decisions.decision: Keep camera, furniture, last-good presentation, point measurements, and annotations in a viewer store keyed by document id; preserve camera and annotations across new geometry, clear measurements only when model identity changes, and compare an optional bridge-computed SHA-256 geometry identity instead of scanning full STL bytes on the UI thread.
decisions.rationale: FR-2.2 and FR-2.9 require per-document continuity and last-good behavior, while high-facet STL byte comparison on the renderer thread conflicts with the responsiveness intent of FR-2.5.

decisions.topic: Demand-driven Three.js lifecycle and off-thread STL ownership
decisions.decision: Lazy-load the 3D runtime, render only on state/control/resize changes, parse defensive STL bytes in a terminable Worker, atomically replace geometry only after parsing succeeds, terminate superseded work, dispose controls and GPU resources explicitly, and use a bounded main-thread fallback only when worker construction is unavailable.
decisions.rationale: This keeps ordinary rendering interactive, avoids endless animation and stale-worker accumulation, preserves the last good mesh during replacement, and makes WebGL/worker absence an honest accessible state rather than a crash.

decisions.topic: Controlled camera commands and C9 viewer-preference authority
decisions.decision: Represent camera position, target, up, zoom, and projection as controlled state; derive reset, six axis views, fit, and projection toggles as pure bounded commands; let C9 profile settings remain the sole durable authority for projection, mouse mapping, furniture defaults, and mesh-color override.
decisions.rationale: A controlled camera makes AC-2.b exact and prevents rerenders from refitting user navigation, while one settings authority avoids divergent persistent and in-memory furniture/projection state.

decisions.decision: Provide independent grid, RGB axes, edges, and ground-shadow controls; hot-apply theme/viewer colors; automatically suppress requested edges and shadow above the large-mesh threshold and disclose the degradation in the viewer; do not claim FR-2.5's 2M-triangle device-class frame rate without a retained hardware run.
decisions.rationale: The implementation can reduce known expensive furniture deterministically, but N-7 forbids converting structural safeguards into an unmeasured performance claim.

decisions.topic: Measurement and annotation scope under open semantics
decisions.decision: Deliver point-to-point distance, exact bounding-box dimensions, labeled/deletable measurement and annotation lists, pointer picking, and model-change clearing; limit annotation text to 240 characters; park edge/face semantics and final keyboard geometry picking under Q-0025/Q-0028 rather than inventing STL topology rules.
decisions.rationale: Point distance and bounds are unambiguous and machine-testable. STL does not preserve authored edge/face topology, and the spec does not define keyboard arbitrary-point selection.

decisions.decision: Persist only annotations in a strict version-1 profile document keyed by exact project identity and normalized project-relative file path; cap the document at 1 MiB, 2,048 file records, 256 annotations per file, reject malformed/case-colliding identities, move/copy/delete keys with C6 file operations, and keep measurements transient.
decisions.rationale: FR-2.7 requires per-file durability outside source. A separate bounded metadata document avoids modifying `.scad`, recovery, or project bytes and supports browser and desktop-WebView profiles uniformly.

decisions.topic: Last-good render, progress, cancellation, and error presentation
decisions.decision: Keep the last successful per-document result visible and dim it while replacement work is active or failed; overlay elapsed time and cancel during a current run; link a non-cancelled failure badge to Console; never let a late background/stale result replace the active document presentation.
decisions.rationale: This encodes FR-2.9 without hiding useful geometry or confusing a stale/background operation with the active document, and reuses the existing C4/C8 cancellation and diagnostic boundaries.

decisions.topic: Scene-only PNG capture and later MCP boundary
decisions.decision: Capture the rendered WebGL canvas as decodable PNG bytes and save through the C6 ArtifactDestination; park DOM-overlay composition under Q-0026 and MCP exposure until C11, while retaining a real Chromium dominant-background acceptance test.
decisions.rationale: Canvas capture is deterministic and covers the rendered viewport/theme. Rasterizing DOM labels/status would require unspecified typography/layout behavior, and C11 owns the MCP surface.

testEvidence.criterion: FR-2.1, FR-2.2, FR-2.5, FR-2.6, FR-2.7, AC-2.b, and AC-2.c pure geometry/state boundaries
testEvidence.test: STL parsing and worker ownership, bounded annotation metadata, all axis/fit/projection camera commands, exact point distance/bounds, per-document isolation, same-geometry identity, last-good presentation, and model-change measurement clearing.

red.outcome: RECONSTRUCTION RED (partial criterion): 3 files failed; 15 tests failed and 14 passed in 2.97 s. Both STL-normal assertions failed because normals were absent, and the bridge assertion failed because geometryIdentity was absent. The broader original camera/annotation/worker-state RED transcript was not retained and is not inferred from file absence.

green.outcome: RECONSTRUCTED C2 GREEN: 6 files and 25 tests passed in 603 ms on the 3D-only C2 stack.

testEvidence.test: ModelViewer, toolbar, details, overlays, camera control, theme hot-apply, atomic mesh replacement, demand rendering, WebGL/worker fallback, point picking, annotation entry, PNG bytes, and superseded-worker termination.

red.outcome: RECONSTRUCTION RED (partial criterion): 3 files failed; 15 tests failed and 14 passed in 2.97 s. ModelViewer failures covered accessible WebGL fallback, stable resize ownership, atomic replacement, demand rendering, controlled camera, picking, PNG capture, worker transfer/fallback, and supersession. Original toolbar/details feature RED output was not retained.

green.outcome: RECONSTRUCTED C2 GREEN: 3 files and 20 tests passed in 1.37 s on the C2 stack.

testEvidence.criterion: FR-2.7 project/file annotation durability through C6 lifecycle integration
testEvidence.test: Annotations survive runtime restart, remain isolated by project and path, move with a renamed/moved file, delete durably, do not persist measurements, and remain usable in memory when metadata writes fail.

red.outcome: HISTORICAL EVIDENCE GAP: remediation notes confirm these cases were originally observed RED, but their exact output/timestamps were not retained. The reconstruction did not mutate the verified C9 parent merely to fabricate a retrospective failure; persistence rejection remains nonfatal and still lacks a visible user warning.

green.outcome: RECONSTRUCTED C2 GREEN: 4 annotation tests passed and 6 unrelated C6 tests were skipped in 650 ms.

testEvidence.criterion: FR-2.1, FR-2.4, FR-2.8, FR-2.10 and FR-9.1/9.2 C9-to-C2 authority seam
testEvidence.test: ViewerPaneConnector uses C9 projection, furniture, mouse, and mesh-color preferences as the live/durable authority and sends captured PNG bytes through the C6 ArtifactDestination.

red.outcome: HISTORICAL EVIDENCE GAP: no original connector RED transcript was retained. The connector file did not exist on the verified C9 parent, but structural absence is not claimed as an assertion-level RED.

green.outcome: RECONSTRUCTED C2 GREEN: 1 file and 2 tests passed in 979 ms on the C9-to-C2 stack.

testEvidence.test: A known 10 mm cube render reaches the composed Workbench viewer, reports 10 × 10 × 10 mm, and discloses preview quality.

red.command: node_modules\.bin\vitest.cmd run tests/ui/Workbench.test.tsx --reporter=verbose -t "renders preview geometry and its measured engine bounds"
red.outcome: NO VALID C2 RED: this acceptance assertion already existed and passed on parent 19899e6, so tests/ui/Workbench.test.tsx is supporting regression evidence and is correctly absent from filesTouched. The production composition changes from LegacyViewerPane to ViewerPaneConnector are covered directly by the C2 pane/connector tests.

green.command: node_modules\.bin\vitest.cmd run tests/ui/Workbench.test.tsx --reporter=verbose -t "renders preview geometry and its measured engine bounds"
green.outcome: SUPPORTING RECONSTRUCTED-C2 REGRESSION: 1 test passed and 14 unrelated tests were skipped in 1.41 s after the real C2 Workbench composition landed.

testEvidence.criterion: AC-2.e real Chromium WebGL viewport PNG
testEvidence.test: A real WebGL canvas capture produces decodable PNG bytes whose dominant color matches the active theme background.

red.outcome: HISTORICAL EVIDENCE GAP: no original assertion-level browser RED transcript was retained. The C2 E2E and fixture were absent on the parent, but file absence is not claimed as a test failure.

green.outcome: RECONSTRUCTED C2 GREEN: 1 Chromium WebGL test passed in 1.8 s with one worker and no retries; it decoded PNG bytes and verified the active theme background dominated the viewport.

testEvidence.test: A deliberately slow $fn=400 minkowski is cancelled in under two seconds, its process is reaped, and the next cube render succeeds.

red.outcome: HISTORICAL EVIDENCE GAP: the original cancellation assertion RED was not retained. A separate unpinned audit selected PATH OpenSCAD 2021.01 and failed a 2D --summary case; that environment mismatch is explicitly not C2 RED.

green.outcome: RECONSTRUCTED C2 GREEN: 1 test passed, 0 failed, and 10 were filtered; the cancellation/recovery body finished in 0.38 s using the probed OpenSCAD 2026.06.12 executable with SHA-256 DE9A0C732C23C3FEB0B49CF938777AA0AEE3E206DB9E98571672CACC4816C524.

testEvidence.criterion: C2-only ViewerPane tests are independent of C3 test order
testEvidence.test: The first C2 pane assertion waits for the genuinely lazy ModelViewer module rather than depending on an earlier C3 routing test to warm it.

red.outcome: RECONSTRUCTION RED: 1 file failed; 1 test failed and 5 passed. The first case synchronously queried data-testid=model-viewer while Suspense still showed Loading model viewer, proving the C2-only test-order dependency.

green.outcome: RECONSTRUCTED C2 GREEN: 1 file and all 6 C2-only ViewerPane tests passed in 1.05 s after the first case became async and awaited findByTestId without preloading production code or weakening behavior.

testEvidence.criterion: V-1/V-2 widened C2 regression and policy accounting
testEvidence.test: Full TypeScript, Chromium, exact-pinned native, desktop Rust, typecheck, lint/source-policy, build, generated-parser, npm-license, Rust formatting, and Rust clippy checks preserve the C9 parent while Q-0001 remains explicit.

red.outcome: EXPECTED POLICY RED: npm license policy passed for 117 packages, then the command exited 1 because 19 Rust packages carry Unicode-3.0. The checker identifies spec/QUESTIONS.md Q-0001; C2 does not waive or conceal the result.

green.command:
pnpm.cmd test
pnpm.cmd exec playwright test --workers=1 --retries=0
$env:SCADMILL_OPENSCAD='<PINNED_2026.06.12_EXE>'; cargo test --manifest-path src/native-engine/Cargo.toml
cargo test --manifest-path src/desktop-shell/src-tauri/Cargo.toml
pnpm.cmd typecheck
pnpm.cmd lint
pnpm.cmd build
pnpm.cmd check:generated
cargo fmt --manifest-path src/native-engine/Cargo.toml -- --check
cargo fmt --manifest-path src/desktop-shell/src-tauri/Cargo.toml -- --check
cargo clippy --manifest-path src/native-engine/Cargo.toml --all-targets -- -D warnings
cargo clippy --manifest-path src/desktop-shell/src-tauri/Cargo.toml --all-targets -- -D warnings
green.outcome: RECONSTRUCTED C2 GREEN OUTSIDE Q-0001: Vitest 92 files/652 tests; Chromium 8/8 with one worker/no retries; exact-pinned native 39/39; desktop Rust 24/24; typecheck, Biome/source-policy, production build, generated parser, both cargo fmt checks, and both all-target clippy runs passed. The npm portion of the license check passed for 117 packages; the separate 19-package Rust policy RED remains recorded above.

questions: Q-0025 remains open: edge-length and face-to-face semantics for triangle meshes are parked; point-to-point distance, bounding-box dimensions, labels, deletion, and model-change clearing continue.
Q-0026 remains open: PNG DOM-overlay composition is parked; deterministic Three.js canvas-scene capture, browser decoding, and C6 desktop/web saving continue. MCP exposure remains C11.
Q-0028 remains open: final keyboard-only arbitrary geometry picking is parked; keyboard camera/screenshot commands, labeled lists, deletion, and pointer picking continue.
FR-2.5's two-million-triangle / 2020-class integrated-GPU >=30 fps target has no retained device-class benchmark. Do not claim it verified; retain it as explicit performance evidence debt.

Annotation metadata write failure is currently nonfatal but silent to the user. Before public release, expose a persistent unsaved-metadata warning/retry state and test denial, recovery, and restart restoration.

The ViewerPane test-order dependency was reproduced as 1 failed / 5 passed and corrected without changing product behavior: the first C2 case now awaits lazy ModelViewer resolution, after which the C2-only file passes 6/6.
Exact reconstructed-stack GREEN outcomes are recorded above. Missing original historical RED transcripts remain disclosed and are not inferred from file absence.
C2 deletes C6's temporary src/ui/viewer/LegacyViewerPane.tsx. C9 separately owns deletion of LegacyWorkbenchStatusBar, which is correctly absent from this entry.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
